issues.body: Reconciliation only extracts a non-empty secret_ref from the issue body. On resume, the secrets preflight and store are skipped, and no API read verifies that the named secret exists in the canonical dispatch-secrets environment. An account issue may outlive a deleted or never-successfully-created secret (including manual recovery/edit scenarios), so this path can validate metadata, merge/add the handle to account_pool, and flip status:available with no usable credential. This violates the required fail-closed behavior for a missing token.
issues.fix_hint: Before setting resume=true, validate the secret_ref shape/handle binding and use an appropriately scoped credential to prove that the exact referenced secret exists in the dispatch-secrets environment. Treat 404, missing authorization, listing/probe failure, or ambiguity as a hard failure. Add a hermetic test that makes the secret probe return 404/error and asserts that no policy PR or activation step is reached.
issues.title: Resume can activate an account whose credential secret no longer exists

verdict.summary: The resume path is not fail-closed with respect to the credential it is about to activate. It treats a syntactically present secret_ref line as proof that the referenced environment secret still exists, skips the store-capability/credential checks, and can consequently open or complete an account_pool change and mark an account available even when its token is missing. A missing token must defer or die in this trust plane. Round 1, so progress is null.
